head: feat/GH-21/confluence-adapter
gate: bun run check = 772 pass / 0 fail; depcruiser clean (74 modules, 101 deps)

findings.title: AC-F5-1(b): /data attachment update is not reachable via the TargetSystem port
findings.description: spec F-5 + AC-F5-1 second clause + §7.1 In Scope require changed-bytes update via /child/attachment/{attId}/data (version bump). AttachmentService.update() implements it and TC-UPD-001 tests it, but ConfluenceTarget wires only uploadAttachment -> attachments.upload; there is no updateAttachment op on the port, and the spec's own F-1 port enumeration omits it. uploadAttachment always POSTs to the create endpoint; on a 400-dup it calls resolveExisting() which returns the EXISTING ref without updating. So a caller using the port (E3-S6 / E4-S1/E4-S2, which spec §13 says depend on 'the port's attachment operations') has no way to perform an in-place /data update. The capability exists but is orphaned. Note the design tension: hash-derived filenames (marksync-mermaid-<hash>.svg) mean changed bytes naturally produce a new filename and thus a fresh create (not a dup), which largely sidesteps the need for /data update — but the spec AC still requires the capability and the tension is unresolved.
findings.suggestedFix: Pick one: (a) add updateAttachment(pageId, attachmentId, artifact) to the TargetSystem port + wire it in ConfluenceTarget so E4 can reach it; OR (b) make uploadAttachment detect a same-hash-name collision with changed bytes and delegate to /data internally so the single upload op is genuinely idempotent; OR (c) explicitly document that hash-derived filenames make /data update unnecessary for MS-0002, remove the orphaned AttachmentService.update + TC-UPD-001, and adjust AC-F5-1(b) accordingly. Record the decision (PD or DEC) whichever path is taken.

findings.title: Hardcoded USER_AGENT version string with no drift guard
findings.description: USER_AGENT = "marksync/0.4.0" happens to match package.json today but will silently desync on the next version bump. The repo review checklist flags hardcoded version strings as a fragile runtime pattern requiring a justifying comment or a safer alternative. The User-Agent header is what Atlassian sees server-side, so a stale value degrades supportability/debuggability.
findings.suggestedFix: Either derive the version from package.json (e.g. a tiny build-time constant or an import that respects tier rules), or add a one-line comment explaining why it is pinned + a drift-guard test asserting USER_AGENT === package.json version.

findings.title: HTTP 401 surfaces as RemoteUnreachable (misleading diagnostic)
findings.description: The client correctly never retries 401 and surfaces it as ok(raw) to the service. PageService.get/update/move then route any non-2xx that isn't 403/404/409 through mapPage, which returns RemoteUnreachable ('unexpected page response status 401'). So an expired/invalid token mid-operation is reported to the user as 'could not reach Confluence (network or server error)'. The spec only mandated 403->Forbidden; 401 is unspecified, so this is not a spec violation, but the message is misleading. (GH-17 validates credentials up front, so a mid-run 401 is unlikely but possible on token expiry.)
findings.suggestedFix: Map a 401 to an auth-shaped error (the existing Auth/InvalidCredentials arm carries a status) or at minimum to a distinct RemoteUnreachable cause like 'authentication rejected (401)' rather than the generic 'unexpected page response status'. Same consideration for properties/attachments/search/restrictions.

findings.title: Retry-After HTTP-date format is unsupported
findings.description: parseRetryAfterMs only parses integer seconds. RFC 7231 also permits Retry-After: <HTTP-date>. Confluence sends seconds, so impact is low, but an HTTP-date value would parse to NaN -> undefined -> silent fallback to exponential backoff, ignoring the server's explicit hint.
findings.suggestedFix: Either accept the limitation with a one-line comment ('Confluence emits integer seconds; HTTP-date Retry-After falls back to exponential backoff'), or add HTTP-date parsing (Date.parse fallback).

findings.title: Duplicated RenderedBody / options types with no structural-compatibility test
findings.description: RenderedBody and RenderBodyOptions are declared independently in src/domain/target/port.ts AND in src/infra/confluence/render/storage.ts (as RenderStorageOptions/RenderedBody). ConfluenceTarget.renderBody delegates renderStorage's Result straight back, relying on TS structural typing for the two shapes to match. The repo review checklist requires duplicated types across tier boundaries to carry a one-line note + a structural compatibility test; neither exists. A future field drift on one side would still compile (structural) but mismatch at runtime.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a one-line comment noting the cross-tier duplication, and a type-level test asserting the port RenderedBody is assignable to/from the storage RenderedBody (or have the port re-export/alias the renderer-owned types if tier rules allow).

findings.title: Boundary probe leak blast-radius: a killed run permanently poisons depcruise src
findings.description: The ephemeral src/domain/__boundary_probe__.ts is created at runtime and removed in afterEach. afterEach runs on pass and fail, but a SIGKILL/OOM/panic between writeFileSync and afterEach leaves the probe on disk, which then makes 'depcruise src' / 'check:boundaries' fail for EVERYONE until the file is manually removed. This is an accepted tradeoff per PD-4, but the blast radius is repo-wide.
findings.suggestedFix: Harden cleanup: a beforeAll that removes any stale probe before the suite starts, AND/OR a process-level exit hook (process.on('beforeExit'/'exit')) that removes it, AND/OR an entry in .gitignore + a depcruise allowed-pattern note so a leaked probe never reaches a commit.

findings.title: Awkward double import of the ConfluenceClient class
findings.description: import { type ConfluenceClient, type ConfluenceClientOptions, ConfluenceClient as Client } imports the class name once as a type-only alias and again as a value aliased to Client, then uses Client(...) in fromCredentials while the field type uses ConfluenceClient. Valid TS, but the two names for one class is mildly confusing.
findings.suggestedFix: Simplify to a single value import (import { ConfluenceClient }) + inline type modifier for the options type, naming the value ConfluenceClient throughout; or alias only when a genuine name clash exists.
